item.title: Britain acts richer than it is
item.description: The country’s habits and virtues are built for a prosperity it no longer enjoys

item.title: The Iran war shock for developing nations
item.description: Poorer countries face a triple whammy of crises in fuel, food and remittances

item.title: Why bank taxes might be back on the table in the UK
item.description: The problem with ‘windfall’ taxes is that the relatively small amount raised is often not worth the long-term cost

item.title: Can AI discriminate if it can’t justify itself?
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawsuit against Colorado raises a deeper philosophical question about artificial intelligence and democracy

item.title: This World Cup is missing an open goal
item.description: And I should know — as Mexican ambassador to the US, I helped to craft the original bid

item.title: Good news for business travellers
item.description: The seats in economy that so many of us use are finally getting an upgrade

item.title: Chart of the Week: The S&P just doesn’t care
item.description: The war is over — in the US stock market, at least

item.title: Jancis Robinson’s A-Z of 21st-century wine
item.description: A guide to what’s new in the wine world

item.title: The mystery of good judgment
item.description: It does more to shape a life than intellect and hard work

item.title: Power is draining away from Starmer’s Downing Street
item.description: Loyalty has worn thin because of the PM’s lack of interest in making the difficult decisions his job requires

item.title: What the AI ‘jobpocalypse’ narrative misses
item.description: Whether or not new technologies can perform a task is only a tiny part of the picture

item.title: No edge, no hedge: why markets are stuck
item.description: Neither extreme optimism nor extreme pessimism makes much sense, and neither is easy nor safe to express

item.title: Have you got a God complex?
item.description: Declaring oneself the new messiah is distinctly on trend

item.title: From Anthropic’s Mythos to the Birkin bag, scarcity sells
item.description: The idea that Mythos is too hot to handle ought to be good for the company’s valuation

item.title: Kallos, Salford: No wonder the UK is going crazy for Greek food — restaurant review
item.description: Even a grey day feels sunny when you’ve got a carafe of wine, herby dips and proper feta

item.title: In pursuit of an itinerant spring
item.description: In some places, the daffodils appear in January — but elsewhere the season is fleeting

item.title: Jancis Robinson’s A-Z of 21st-century wine — part three
item.description: From Scandinavia to Zuccardi, via Ukraine and ‘young people’

item.title: Boots hopes its upcoming IPO will be bigger, bolder, more beautiful
item.description: UK retailer has grown sales at a steady 6% annually over the past five years

item.title: The narrow path to a US-Iran deal
item.description: An uneasy ceasefire cannot be the long-term solution in the Gulf

item.title: Private credit won’t spark the next financial crisis
item.description: A lower leverage ratio and limited links to banks mean that the sector is unlikely to pose a systemic threat

item.title: The Apple juggernaut and the AI roadblock
item.description: John Ternus will face innovation challenges that Tim Cook never had to

item.title: The golden age of arbitrage has begun
item.description: The law of one price is in retreat — with major consequences for profits, inflation and innovation

item.title: Big Four’s shrinkage at the top presages weakness at the bottom line
item.description: Professional service firms appear to be braced for flatlining growth

item.title: Mandelson row is a cold shower for civil service relations
item.description: The fallout from an ambassadorial appointment like no other threatens even worse procedural ‘sludge’ in Whitehall

item.title: Why do I have so much in UK equities?
item.description: British pension schemes will soon be forced to support my portfolio
